binaries.title: mrxsmb.sys - No security-relevant change (feature-staged SMB-over-QUIC compression message routing)

changed_functions.name: SmbQuicReceiveEvent (0x1c0046fe4 unpatched / 0x1c0047074 patched)
key_changes: Replaced the inline 4-byte peek-and-compare (compare against 0x424d53fd / \xfdSMB TRANSFORM_HEADER) with a call to the new helper SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage (0x1c0046e3c)

The helper reads 4 bytes at the current offset and, ONLY when Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (0x1c002110c) returns non-zero, also matches 0x424d53fc / \xfcSMB COMPRESSION_TRANSFORM_HEADER via (value - 0x424d53fc) <= 1; when the feature is disabled it checks only 0x424d53fd, reproducing the unpatched behavior exactly
Stack frame grew from 0xB8 to 0xC8 (16 bytes)
All other variance is register/stack-slot renumbering from the larger frame and recompilation-induced address/symbol shifts
changed_functions.security_notes: SmbQuicReceiveEvent is on the SMB-over-QUIC client receive/decode path (QUIC/UDP transport, not TCP/445). In its large/fragmented-message branch it peeks the first 4 bytes of the pending message body to decide whether the message is a 'transform header' message that must be reassembled into a contiguous non-paged pool buffer before processing, versus an ordinary message copied inline into the fixed receive buffer at [ctx+0x88] and dispatched to the SMB2 handler at (*(ctx+0x168)+0x10). The unpatched build routes only 0x424d53fd (\xfdSMB, TRANSFORM_HEADER/encrypted) to the reassembly path; the patched build additionally routes 0x424d53fc (\xfcSMB, COMPRESSION_TRANSFORM_HEADER) to that path, but ONLY when the Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix feature flag is enabled, retaining the original single-magic behavior as the fallback branch. This is a staged rollout of an SMB-over-QUIC compression routing correction, not an unconditionally delivered change, and it has no demonstrable memory-safety impact: the inline path copies min(remaining_in_fragment, 0x400) bytes into the fixed receive buffer with a matched length, then dispatches the message to the SMB2 parser, which validates the protocol id (a body beginning with 0xfc 'SMB' is neither valid SMB2 0xfe 'SMB' nor valid transform 0xfd 'SMB'). No out-of-bounds access, controlled write, use-after-free, information leak, or control-flow hijack is present in either build. The address 0x1c002110c in the unpatched binary is an unrelated ETW template (McTemplateK0dhsr1qqbr4qbr6hzr8qbr10_EtwWriteTransfer); the feature-staging gate at that address exists only in the patched build and was matched by content.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- UNPATCHED (SmbQuicReceiveEvent @ 0x1c0046fe4, inline peek/compare):
00000001C0047337  mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+Mdl]
00000001C004733B  lea     rdx, [rbp+57h+var_80]
00000001C004733F  and     dword ptr [rbp+57h+var_80], r15d   ; zero local
00000001C0047343  mov     r9d, 4
00000001C004734E  mov     r8d, r14d                          ; current offset
00000001C0047351  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx    ; peek 4 bytes
00000001C0047356  cmp     dword ptr [rbp+57h+var_80], 424D53FDh  ; only \xfdSMB
00000001C004735D  jz      loc_1C00474A3                      ; -> reassembly path
00000001C0047363  mov     rbx, [rdi+88h]                     ; fixed receive buffer
00000001C004736A  mov     r9d, r13d                          ; length capped to 0x400
00000001C004737C  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx    ; length-matched copy
00000001C0047389  mov     rax, [rdi+168h]
00000001C00473CD  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr     ; SMB2 dispatch

--- PATCHED (SmbQuicReceiveEvent @ 0x1c0047074):
00000001C00473C7  mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+var_88]
00000001C00473CB  mov     edx, r14d                          ; current offset
00000001C00473CE  call    SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage
00000001C00473D3  test    al, al
00000001C00473D5  jnz     loc_1C004751B                      ; \xfdSMB, and \xfcSMB when feature on
00000001C00473DB  mov     rbx, [rdi+88h]

--- NEW HELPER SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage @ 0x1c0046e3c:
00000001C0046E3C  sub     rsp, 38h
00000001C0046E45  mov     r8d, edx
00000001C0046E4E  lea     rdx, [rsp+38h+arg_10]
00000001C0046E53  mov     r9d, 4
00000001C0046E59  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx    ; peek 4 bytes
00000001C0046E5E  call    Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
00000001C0046E63  test    eax, eax
00000001C0046E65  jz      short loc_1C0046E78                ; feature off -> \xfdSMB only
00000001C0046E67  mov     eax, [rsp+38h+arg_10]
00000001C0046E6B  add     eax, 0BDB2AC04h                    ; value - 0x424d53fc
00000001C0046E70  cmp     eax, 1
00000001C0046E73  setbe   al                                ; 0x424d53fc OR 0x424d53fd
00000001C0046E76  jmp     short loc_1C0046E83
00000001C0046E78  cmp     [rsp+38h+arg_10], 424D53FDh
00000001C0046E80  setz    al                                ; 0x424d53fd only
00000001C0046E83  add     rsp, 38h
00000001C0046E87  retn

security_findings.function: SmbQuicReceiveEvent (SMB-over-QUIC stream receive/decode handler, 0x1c0046fe4 unpatched / 0x1c0047074 patched)
security_findings.entry_point: SMB-over-QUIC client receive path (QUIC/UDP transport). Real call chain: SmbQuicClientStreamCallback (0x1c006c7b0, MsQuic stream event callback) -> SmbQuicStreamEventRecv (0x1c0047cd0) -> SmbQuicReceiveEvent.
call_chain: SmbQuicReceiveEvent (0x1c0046fe4 unpatched / 0x1c0047074 patched) - stream receive/decode handler
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change; feature-staged SMB-over-QUIC compression message routing correction
security_findings.description: In the large/fragmented-message branch of SmbQuicReceiveEvent, the code peeks the first 4 bytes of the pending message body to classify it as a transform-header message (routed to the buffered reassembly path: ExAllocatePool2 + IoAllocateMdl + MmBuildMdlForNonPagedPool) or an ordinary message (copied inline into the fixed receive buffer at [ctx+0x88] and dispatched to the SMB2 handler at (*(ctx+0x168)+0x10)). The unpatched build compares the peeked value against a single constant 0x424d53fd (\xfdSMB, TRANSFORM_HEADER). The patched build replaces the inline compare with a call to the new helper SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage (0x1c0046e3c), which performs the same peek and, ONLY when Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (0x1c002110c) returns non-zero, additionally matches 0x424d53fc (\xfcSMB, COMPRESSION_TRANSFORM_HEADER) via the range test (value - 0x424d53fc) <= 1. When the feature is disabled, the helper checks only 0x424d53fd and is byte-for-byte equivalent to the unpatched behavior. Because the added recognition is gated behind a WIL feature-staging flag with the prior behavior retained as the else branch, this is a staged rollout, not an unconditionally delivered change. It carries no demonstrable memory-safety impact.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) SMB-over-QUIC transport in use with SMB 3.1.1 compression negotiated. 2) Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix enabled (otherwise the patched helper equals the unpatched compare). 3) A server response body beginning with \xfcSMB (0x424d53fc) that takes the large/fragmented-message branch (message size exceeds in-fragment remaining bytes or 0x400). 4) byte_1C005DA0C & 8 not set (present identically in both builds; when set, both builds skip the peek and route to the reassembly path). Under these conditions the unpatched build dispatches the compressed fragment inline while the patched build (feature on) routes it to reassembly. No memory-corruption outcome is demonstrated for either.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The inline path copies min(remaining_in_fragment, 0x400) bytes into the fixed receive buffer [ctx+0x88] with a matched length and passes that length plus the byte-swapped message size to the SMB2 dispatcher, which validates the protocol id. No out-of-bounds read/write, controlled pointer dereference, use-after-free, information leak, or control-flow hijack is present in either build. The worst realistic consequence of the unpatched routing is a functional/interoperability error (a compressed SMB-over-QUIC response handed to the ordinary SMB2 parser and rejected).
security_findings.assembly:
SmbQuicReceiveEvent - inline peek/compare (UNPATCHED mrxsmb_unpatched.sys @ 0x1c0046fe4):

; Large/fragmented-message branch entry:
00000001C0047238  mov     r13d, r12d
00000001C004723B  mov     eax, 400h
00000001C0047240  sub     r13d, r14d              ; remaining in fragment
00000001C0047243  cmp     esi, eax                ; message size vs 0x400
00000001C0047245  ja      loc_1C0047316           ; large -> peek path
00000001C004724B  cmp     esi, r13d               ; message size vs remaining
00000001C004724E  ja      loc_1C0047316           ; spans fragment -> peek path

; Peek path:
00000001C0047316  cmp     r13d, eax
00000001C0047319  jbe     short loc_1C0047320
00000001C004731B  mov     r13d, eax               ; r13d = min(remaining, 0x400)
00000001C0047320  cmp     r13d, 4
00000001C0047324  jb      loc_1C00474A3           ; < 4 -> reassembly path
00000001C004732A  test    cs:byte_1C005DA0C, 8
00000001C0047331  jnz     loc_1C00474A3           ; config gate -> reassembly path
00000001C0047337  mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+Mdl]
00000001C004733B  lea     rdx, [rbp+57h+var_80]
00000001C004733F  and     dword ptr [rbp+57h+var_80], r15d
00000001C0047343  mov     r9d, 4
00000001C004734E  mov     r8d, r14d
00000001C0047351  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx   ; peek 4 bytes
00000001C0047356  cmp     dword ptr [rbp+57h+var_80], 424D53FDh  ; only \xfdSMB
00000001C004735D  jz      loc_1C00474A3           ; -> reassembly path
00000001C0047363  mov     rbx, [rdi+88h]          ; fixed receive buffer
00000001C004736A  mov     r9d, r13d               ; length capped to 0x400
00000001C004737C  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx   ; length-matched copy
00000001C0047381  test    eax, eax
00000001C0047383  js      loc_1C0047450
00000001C0047389  mov     rax, [rdi+168h]
00000001C00473CD  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr    ; SMB2 dispatch

; Reassembly path (both builds, reached for \xfdSMB and short/gated messages):
00000001C00474A3  mov     r8d, 66426D53h          ; pool tag
00000001C00474AC  mov     ecx, 42h
00000001C00474B1  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePool2
00000001C00474E5  call    cs:__imp_IoAllocateMdl
00000001C0047521  call    cs:__imp_MmBuildMdlForNonPagedPool

; NEW HELPER SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage (PATCHED @ 0x1c0046e3c):
00000001C0046E3C  sub     rsp, 38h
00000001C0046E45  mov     r8d, edx
00000001C0046E4E  lea     rdx, [rsp+38h+arg_10]
00000001C0046E53  mov     r9d, 4
00000001C0046E59  call    SmbQuicCopyIndicationToBufferEx
00000001C0046E5E  call    Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
00000001C0046E63  test    eax, eax
00000001C0046E65  jz      short loc_1C0046E78
00000001C0046E67  mov     eax, [rsp+38h+arg_10]
00000001C0046E6B  add     eax, 0BDB2AC04h          ; value - 0x424d53fc
00000001C0046E70  cmp     eax, 1
00000001C0046E73  setbe   al                       ; 0x424d53fc OR 0x424d53fd
00000001C0046E76  jmp     short loc_1C0046E83
00000001C0046E78  cmp     [rsp+38h+arg_10], 424D53FDh
00000001C0046E80  setz    al                       ; 0x424d53fd only
00000001C0046E83  add     rsp, 38h
00000001C0046E87  retn

observation_points.why: The 4-byte peek of the message body. Unpatched: inline compare of the peeked value against 0x424d53fd immediately follows at 0x1c0047356. Patched: the call to SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage classifies the message. Inspect the peeked 4 bytes to see which branch is taken. This is a routing observation, not a crash trigger.

The feature-staging gate call inside SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage. When Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix is disabled the helper returns identical results to the unpatched inline compare (0x424d53fd only).

debugging_aids.notes: 0x424d53fd and 0x424d53fc are the MS-SMB2 TRANSFORM_HEADER (0xfd 'SMB') and COMPRESSION_TRANSFORM_HEADER (0xfc 'SMB') protocol ids. The change extends recognition to the compression id on the SMB-over-QUIC receive path, gated by a feature-staging flag with the prior behavior retained as fallback. No memory-corruption outcome (BSOD, pool corruption, controlled write) is demonstrable from either build; the reproduction value here is confirming the routing difference, not a crash.

summary: The patch is a feature-staged SMB-over-QUIC compression message-routing correction in mrxsmb.sys, not a security-relevant memory-safety fix. In SmbQuicReceiveEvent (0x1c0046fe4 unpatched / 0x1c0047074 patched), the large/fragmented-message branch peeks the first 4 bytes of the message body to route transform-header messages to the buffered reassembly path versus dispatching ordinary messages inline. The unpatched build recognizes only 0x424d53fd (\xfdSMB, TRANSFORM_HEADER). The patched build replaces the inline compare with the new helper SmbQuicIsIndicationTransformedMessage (0x1c0046e3c), which additionally recognizes 0x424d53fc (\xfcSMB, COMPRESSION_TRANSFORM_HEADER) ONLY when Feature_Servicing_SmbCompressionOverQuicFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (0x1c002110c) returns non-zero, retaining the original single-magic behavior as fallback. No memory-corruption primitive is demonstrable: the inline path performs a length-matched, 0x400-capped copy into a fixed receive buffer and hands the message to the SMB2 dispatcher, which validates the protocol id. An independent function-by-function comparison of both builds confirms this is the only logic change; all other differences are relocation churn (shifted call targets, renamed WPP trace symbols, locret label addresses). Final assessment: no security-relevant change (severity none).
